mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recessionary pressures, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in the consumer price index indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling period, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary easing reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations during easing cycles, or the correlation coefficient between INDPRO growth and SPX returns is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls exceeding a 150.0 thousand increase signals strong labor market expansion and rising consumer income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150.0 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months following payrolls above 150.0 thousand, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently high federal funds rates and rising consumer prices indicate restrictive monetary policy amid inflationary pressure, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term shows no predictive power in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during periods of stable unemployment reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns when UNRATE remains below 4.5%, or the signal accuracy falls below 45% in this specific regime.
